analysis.summary: The All-In Podcast discusses the socialist sweep in NYC congressional primaries, the rise of socialism in the US, and the progress of Chinese open-source AI models. The hosts also cover the future of data centers, the challenges of standing up new data centers, and the potential for orbital compute. Additionally, they talk about recent IPOs, including SpaceX and Cerebrus, and how the market will absorb the new inventory.

takeaways.text: Mamdani-endorsed socialists sweep congressional primaries in NYC
The DSA's voting base consists of relatively wealthy, white liberals who are downwardly mobile.
China's GLM 5.2 model is now as good as the currently available models from OpenAI and Anthropic
Micron's earnings smash expectations, with revenue up 9 billion to 42 billion, and their stock is up 10x
Standing up a new data center can take 2-3 years and cost $35 billion in semiconductors and $25 billion in power and cooling equipment
Dario Amodei's strategy may have been to provoke the US government into regulating AI, giving Anthropic a regulatory moat
Israel has a PR problem and needs a young, fluent spokesperson
